text:
INDICES — VIX 25.3, down 1.86% on the day (52-week range 13.5–52.3). Today's return ranking: IWM +1.22%, QQQ +0.66%, DIA +0.64%, SPY +0.56%. Small caps outperformed large caps. TLT gained +0.96% as Treasury yields fell, supporting the risk-on tone, while the dollar edged up +0.18%.

SECTORS — XLB +1.98%, XLV +1.00%, XLY +0.96%, XLI +0.67%, XLP +0.49%, XLK +0.45%, XLU +0.35%, XLC +0.28%, XLF +0.12%, XLRE -0.05%, XLE -0.44%. Top industries: REMX +5.35% and WGMI +3.68%. Bottom industries: BOAT -1.26% and MSOS -1.32%. Energy's -0.44% drop tied directly to WTI crude falling -1.00% as lower oil prices pressured Exxon and Chevron.

THE 7s — Semi 7 +3.64%, New Metals 7 +2.12%, Old Metals 7 +2.03%, Defense 7 +1.12%, Health 7 +1.09%, Buildout 7 +1.03%, Housing 7 +0.94%, Old Staples 7 +0.78%, Freight 7 +0.78%, Bank 7 +0.70%, Industrial 7 +0.65%, Cloud 7 +0.58%, New Staples 7 +0.45%, Power 7 +0.40%, Medtech 7 +0.36%, Energy 7 +0.08%, Insurance 7 -0.15%, Toll Road 7 -0.22%, Fab 7 -0.92%, Software 7 -1.17%. Strongest ticker in Semi 7: AMD +7%. Weakest in Software 7: Micron -3.4%.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 51.5, Neutral. Vol spikes: IJK 16.9x, IJS 12.8x, XHE 4.4x. 20-day trend leader: Energy 7 +13.13%; laggard: Old Metals 7 -19.17%. Best sector 20d: XLE +10.39%; worst: XLP -8.43%. Semiconductor strength was driven by softer CPI (-0.17pp to 2.66%) lifting growth sentiment, with AMD and Intel each surging 7%.
